message:
fix(demo): persist pane workspace even when overlay sync fails

The showcase demo's pane-overlay sync layer disabled itself on the
first error from `paneStateSnapshot`, `paneExportWorkspaceSnapshot`,
or `paneImportWorkspaceSnapshot` (`disablePaneOverlaySync` sets
`paneOverlaySyncFailed = true`). After that flag flipped, both
`persistPaneWorkspace` and `restorePaneWorkspace` early-returned
`false` on every subsequent call, so any pane edits made after the
sync went bad were silently dropped on tab close. Worse, an export
failure was treated as a hard, permanent failure even though it
might just be a transient WASM panic on a single state snapshot.

Three coordinated changes to make pane-workspace persistence
robust to overlay-sync trouble:

1. Decouple persistence from `paneOverlaySyncFailed`:
   - `persistPaneWorkspace` no longer returns early on
     `paneOverlaySyncFailed`. The two real preconditions are
     `runner.paneExportWorkspaceSnapshot` and
     `runner.paneMarkWorkspaceSaved` being callable; if those are
     present we attempt the save.
   - `restorePaneWorkspace` no longer short-circuits on
     `paneOverlaySyncFailed`. A working
     `paneImportWorkspaceSnapshot` is sufficient to restore on
     reload, and a snapshot import failure is logged as a warning
     instead of permanently disabling the path.

2. Snapshot-export failures become a retry, not a kill switch:
   - On `paneExportWorkspaceSnapshot` throw, schedule the next save
     attempt at `now + PANE_WORKSPACE_SAVE_RETRY_MS` and surface a
     one-shot user-visible warning via `warnPaneWorkspaceSaveFailure`.
     The next state change will try again. Same for
     `paneImportWorkspaceSnapshot`: log and move on.
   - `syncPaneOverlayFromRunner` now calls
     `persistPaneWorkspaceIfChanged(null)` both at the start (when
     overlay sync is already disabled) and in its catch arm (just
     after `disablePaneOverlaySync`). That gives the user one last
     save attempt off the most recent in-runtime state before the
     overlay path goes quiet.

3. Pointer / generation lookup falls back to runner methods so a
   bad `state` doesn't masquerade as "no work to save":
   - New `paneWorkspaceSavedGenerationText(state)` mirrors the
     existing `paneWorkspaceGenerationText(state)` helper — checks
     `state.saved_generation` first, then `runner.paneSaved-
     WorkspaceGeneration()` if available, then "". Used by the
     dirty check.
   - `paneWorkspaceIsDirty(state)` is rewritten to compare the two
     text-form helpers (so a runner-side saved generation still
     counts even when `state.saved_generation` is null).
   - New `paneWorkspaceHasActivePointer(state)` checks
     `state.active_pointer_id`, falls through to
     `runner.paneActivePointerId()`, and finally to the local
     `paneActivePointers.size > 0` map. Used by
     `persistPaneWorkspaceIfChanged` so an interaction in flight
     blocks the save through any of the three signal sources.

Net effect: the demo no longer permanently loses workspace
persistence after a single transient runtime hiccup, and a save
attempt still fires on the way out when overlay sync gives up.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
